name: Shatranj
author: AGP
description: Shatranj, medieval Persian/Arabic chess and the ancestor of the modern game, on an 8x8 board. Slow short-range pieces: the Firzan (queen) steps one square diagonally, the Alfil (bishop) leaps exactly two squares diagonally, pawns make no double step and promote only to a Firzan, and there is no castling. You win by checkmate, by stalemating the opponent (a stalemate is a WIN here, not a draw), or by baring the enemy king (reducing them to a lone king) unless they can bare yours in reply. White (player 0) moves first.
